info.name: Invo.ai API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Invo.ai — AI-powered document generation platform.

Variables used:
- `base_url`      : http://localhost:3000 (dev) or https://clorefy.com (prod)
- `auth_token`    : Supabase JWT access token (from browser cookie / login response)
- `session_id`    : Active document_session UUID
- `client_id`     : Client record UUID
- `user_id`       : Auth user UUID
- `brevo_api_key` : Brevo API key for direct Brevo health-check requests

Sections:
🔐 Auth & CSRF · 📄 Sessions · 🤖 AI · 📧 Emails · 💳 Payments
✍️ Signatures · 👥 Clients · 🗂️ Storage · 👤 Profile · 📊 Usage
🔄 Recurring · 💬 Quotations · 🔔 Onboarding · 📨 Brevo CRM Sync
💰 Razorpay · 🆘 Support · 🛡️ Admin

variable.description: Brevo API key (xkeysib-...). Set this to test the Brevo health check directly. Corresponds to BREVO_API_KEY env var.

request.description: Fetch a fresh HMAC-signed CSRF token for state-changing requests.
Create a new document session. Returns session ID, type, and status.
Create a child document linked to a parent session (e.g. payment follow-up from invoice).

Permanently deletes a document session owned by the authenticated user.

Passes sessionId as a query parameter (not a request body).

Protected statuses (paid, signed) cannot be deleted — these are legal/financial records.
No rate limiting — low-frequency action protected by auth + RLS ownership check.

Returns:
- 200: { success: true }
- 400: Invalid session ID format
- 403: Session is paid or signed
- 404: Session not found or not owned by user
Classifies user intent to determine document type and routing (chat-only vs direct-create).

exec: pm.test('Has type field', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('type'); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('route'); });

request.description: Main AI generation endpoint. Returns Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream with activity, delta, and complete events.

formdata.value: Extract client details from this file

request.description: Analyze an uploaded file (PDF, image) to extract client/business details.

body.raw:
{
  "sessionId": "{{session_id}}",
  "recipientEmail": "client@example.com",
  "recipientName": "John Doe",
  "subject": "Invoice from Invo",
  "message": "Please find your invoice attached."
}

request.description: Send a document via email with optional payment link.

exec: pm.test('Has valid field', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('valid'); });

request.description: Webhook endpoint for email delivery status updates from Mailtrap.

exec: pm.test('Has signatures array', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('signatures'); });
pm.test('Has clients array', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j).to.have.property('clients'); });

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Acme Corporation",
  "email": "billing@acme.com",
  "phone": "+1 555 0100",
  "address": "123 Main St, New York, NY 10001",
  "taxId": "12-3456789"
}

exec: pm.test('Has client.id', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); if (j.client) pm.collectionVariables.set('client_id', j.client.id); });

request.description: Upload a file (logo, signature image) to Cloudflare R2.
Public endpoint — client accepts or rejects a quotation/proposal via their unique token.

item.name: 📨 Brevo CRM Sync
item.description: Internal Brevo contact sync calls. These are triggered automatically from auth callbacks and onboarding — not called directly by the frontend. Documented here for observability and debugging.

request.description: Creates or updates a Brevo contact on login. Called from /auth/callback. New users land in the Onboarding Started list (list ID from BREVO_ONBOARDING_LIST_ID). Completed users land in Active Users (BREVO_ACTIVE_LIST_ID). Sets LAST_ACTIVE, FIRSTNAME, SIGNUP_AT attributes.

exec: // This is an internal call — 200 or fire-and-forget 204 are both acceptable

request.description: Calling /api/onboarding/track with phase='completed' triggers syncOnboardingComplete() which: (1) moves the contact from Onboarding Started → Active Users list, (2) sets ONBOARDING_COMPLETE=true and LAST_ACTIVE=today.

body.raw:
{
  "sessionId": "{{session_id}}",
  "recipientEmail": "client@example.com",
  "recipientName": "John Doe",
  "subject": "Invoice from Clorefy",
  "message": "Please find your invoice attached."
}

request.description: The email send route internally calls sendTransactionalEmail() from lib/brevo.ts. Sends via Brevo SMTP API to noreply@clorefy.com. Returns {success, messageId} on 200. Returns 429 if email limit exceeded for the user's tier.
Direct ping to Brevo API to verify the BREVO_API_KEY is valid and the account is active. Use this to debug Brevo connectivity issues. Expects 200 with account details.
Verify the admin PIN to get a temporary admin session.

Returns full user detail for admin drawer.

Response shape (key fields):
- profile: profiles row
- business: businesses row
- usageStats: user_usage row for current month + extra all-time fields:
    - documents_count_all_time (from generation_history WHERE success=true)
    - emails_count_all_time (from document_emails)
- recentDocuments: last 10 rows from generation_history (fields: id, document_type, prompt, success, tokens_used, generation_time_ms, created_at, session_id, title)
    NOTE: source changed from 'documents' table to 'generation_history' table
- recentEmails: last 10 document_emails rows (fields: id, document_type, status, created_at, recipient_email, subject)
    NOTE: 'subject' field added
- recentAuditLogs: last 20 audit_log rows
- totalEmailsSent: all-time email count (integer)
